task_completion.description: Agent modified the required preferences in Firefox about:config
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Firefox browser open to the 'about:config' page, with the agent searching for and modifying the specified network, privacy, media, geo, or webgl preferences.

task_completion.description: Agent set Enhanced Tracking Protection to Strict
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Firefox 'Settings' -> 'Privacy & Security' page with the 'Strict' option selected under Enhanced Tracking Protection.

task_completion.description: Agent set the homepage to MDN Web Docs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Firefox 'Settings' -> 'Home' page with 'Custom URLs...' selected and 'https://developer.mozilla.org' entered in the text box.

task_completion.description: Agent created the JSON configuration audit report at the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (e.g., nano, gedit, VS Code) or terminal command creating/saving a file specifically at '~/Documents/browser_security_config.json' or '/home/ga/Documents/browser_security_config.json'.

task_completion.description: JSON report contains the correct audit date and policy name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible JSON content includes a date in YYYY-MM-DD format and a key for the policy name exactly matching 'Fintech Browser Hardening Baseline'.

task_completion.description: JSON report contains the about_config_changes object with all 8 preferences
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible JSON content includes an 'about_config_changes' object containing the 8 required preferences (network.dns.disablePrefetch, network.prefetch-next, media.peerconnection.enabled, geo.enabled, privacy.resistFingerprinting, network.http.sendRefererHeader, browser.send_pings, webgl.disabled) with their corresponding hardened values.

task_completion.description: JSON report documents the tracking protection and homepage settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible JSON content includes keys/values indicating the tracking protection status is 'strict' and the homepage URL is 'https://developer.mozilla.org'.

integrity.description: Agent actually configured the browser rather than only writing the JSON report
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing actual interaction with the Firefox GUI (about:config, settings) or terminal commands modifying Firefox profile files (prefs.js). The agent must not skip straight to writing the JSON file.

integrity.description: The JSON report was actually saved to the file system
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the JSON content inside a real application window (like a text editor) or being output in the terminal via a command like 'cat'. It should not just be text generated in the agent's chat interface.

integrity.description: The output file is formatted as valid JSON
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the browser_security_config.json file uses proper JSON syntax (curly braces, quoted keys, appropriate boolean/integer/string values) rather than just being a plain text list copied from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying and documenting the following 8 Firefox preferences: network.dns.disablePrefetch (true), network.prefetch-next (false), media.peerconnection.enabled (false), geo.enabled (false), privacy.resistFingerprinting (true), network.http.sendRefererHeader (0), browser.send_pings (false), webgl.disabled (true). Enhanced Tracking Protection must be 'strict' and the homepage must be 'https://developer.mozilla.org'. The output file must be located at /home/ga/Documents/browser_security_config.json.
